steps.title: Sketch the L-shaped gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch an L-shape for the overall pose — a vertical stroke for the torso, bending at the hip into a horizontal stroke for the thighs resting on the bench. The bend is the whole posture; get it in one easy stroke.

steps.title: Place the head and note the lean (pencil)
steps.description: Add a small oval at the top of the torso line for the head. Notice how a resting figure's head tips slightly forward, ahead of the spine — let the oval sit just in front of the vertical, not directly above it.

steps.title: Rough in the lower legs and arms (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch the lower legs as two parallel lines dropping from the knee — straight down or angled slightly back. Rough in the arms: one resting on the lap, one on the armrest. Each arm is just a single stroke.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the main silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the head, then draw the torso and thighs as a connected, relaxed shape — the figure should read as one quiet mass, not a collection of separate parts.

steps.title: Ink the lower legs, feet, and arms (ink)
steps.description: Add the lower legs and feet, then the arms. Keep the line quality unhurried — this person is at rest. A slightly slower, softer ink stroke reads differently than an energetic one, and that difference tells the story.
